guardrail: Article identifiers and titles are reproduced as auditable references; full regulatory text is not embedded — link out to the official source.

articles.title: Logical and physical access controls
articles.rationale: Direct mapping: control identifier matches the AICPA SOC 2 TSC CC6.1 criterion verbatim.

articles.title: Monitoring controls (security monitoring + anomaly detection)
articles.rationale: Direct mapping: control identifier matches the AICPA SOC 2 TSC CC7.2 criterion verbatim.

articles.title: Organizational risk tolerance and AI system risks documented
articles.rationale: AI RMF 1.0 MAP function, category 1, subcategory 5: organizational risk tolerance documented.

articles.title: AI system security and resilience measured (incl. runtime policy)
articles.rationale: AI RMF 1.0 MEASURE function, category 2, subcategory 7: security and resilience of the AI system are evaluated and documented.

articles.title: Access control
articles.rationale: Annex A control A.5.15 (Access control). ISO 27001:2022 reorganised the prior A.9 controls into the consolidated A.5.15.

articles.title: Monitoring activities
articles.rationale: Annex A control A.8.16 (Monitoring activities) — continuous detection of anomalous behaviour.

articles.title: ICT readiness for business continuity
articles.rationale: Annex A control A.5.30 — ICT continuity is planned, implemented, maintained, and tested.

articles.title: Information access management
articles.rationale: 45 CFR §164.308(a)(4) — Information access management standard.

articles.title: Audit controls
articles.rationale: 45 CFR §164.312(b) — Implement hardware, software, or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity.

articles.title: Transmission security
articles.rationale: 45 CFR §164.312(e) — Guard against unauthorized access to ePHI transmitted over an electronic communications network.

articles.title: Restrict access to system components and cardholder data by business need to know
articles.rationale: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 7 — Restrict access by business need to know (sub-requirements 7.1–7.3).

articles.title: Log and monitor all access to system components and cardholder data
articles.rationale: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 10 — Log and monitor all access (sub-requirements 10.1–10.7).

articles.title: Test security of systems and networks regularly
articles.rationale: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 11 — Regular testing (sub-requirements 11.1–11.6, vulnerability scans + pen tests).

articles.title: Security of processing
articles.rationale: GDPR Art. 32 — Controllers and processors implement appropriate technical and organisational measures.

articles.title: Records of processing activities
articles.rationale: GDPR Art. 30 — Each controller maintains a record of processing activities under its responsibility.

articles.title: Notification of a personal data breach to the supervisory authority
articles.rationale: GDPR Art. 33 — Notification within 72 hours of becoming aware of a personal data breach.

articles.title: Risk management system
articles.rationale: EU AI Act Art. 9 — Risk management system for high-risk AI systems must be established, implemented, documented and maintained.

articles.title: Data and data governance
articles.rationale: EU AI Act Art. 10 — Training, validation and testing data sets shall be subject to data governance and management practices.

articles.title: Record-keeping
articles.rationale: EU AI Act Art. 12 — High-risk AI systems shall technically allow automatic recording of events (logs) over the lifetime of the system.

articles.title: AI risk treatment
articles.rationale: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 clause 6.1.3 — define and apply an AI risk treatment process with options, controls, and a Statement of Applicability.

articles.title: AI system impact assessment process
articles.rationale: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 clause 8.2 — operational planning and control for the AI system across its lifecycle, with impact assessment.
